ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: a nyɩ yɩm yar, k'ɩ tɔ ka Yeezuu, bala, a m n'a do bɩ bʊmbɔrɛ a mimbʊnyaarɔ nɔ ʊ.»
translated: and he will see the light, and he will know Jesus, because, he is the one who will give him his salvation and his life with him."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: When Herod saw that the wise men had tricked him, he became very angry. He sent and killed all the male children in Bethlehem and in all that region who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had learned from the wise men. But the wise men, having been warned in a dream, they did not return to him but went back to their country by another way.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: N zeedarɛ ma Ramaa ʊ, nyikəre kan heerzarlɛ gʊta kɩ: Rasɛl nɩ nyi karɛ a nyɩnɔ minto, a lɛ baa kʊ n n'a wɔ zɩnzɛ a nɩ y, nyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ gɩnga bɩ tɔ ma. Zozɛf bɔ Eziptɩ ʊ
translated: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning: Rachel weeping for her children, and she refused to be comforted because they are no more. Joseph in Egypt

text: Zozɛf n wuti, an nyɩ bɩ kan a da bɩ kɩ gaa, an ta Israyɛl ʊ.
translated: Joseph is dead, and we see that his father is also dead, and Israel too.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: A n aa nyɩnta kʊ kʊ n n'a hɩm ma Nazarɛtɩ ʊ b'ʊ, k'an bɩr yɩrɛ ɩ ʊ hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ a hɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ, ŋ ʊ: N y'a birm Nazarɛtɩ gʊaa.
translated: And when they wanted to take him to Nazareth where he was, so that they could show him to the people on the day of the Sabbath, he said: I will not enter Nazareth again.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Yeezuu k'a batɛm bɩ si bɩ, a bɔ hi b'ʊ. A m bɩ, brama n a gʊ, an Woso Ɛspri yɩ an nɩ zerle, am cirpʊʊrɛ hɔ bɩ m an bɩ zer la.
translated: Jesus did not come to condemn us, he came to save us. If we believe, God will give us his Holy Spirit to help us, and we will have strength to do his work.

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.

translated: If you do not believe me, you will not understand this: You will not understand it at all, because, I am God's messenger.

translated: If someone asks you: Why do they take the colt that has never been ridden, tell them... they need it." The disciples

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: «N y'a ya, n n a hɩ sɔ, n ʊ: ‹Kʊ gʊaa la a lʊ ra k'an wɔsaalʊmaci ka ʊ.›
translated: "If I had known, I would have told you: 'Don't let him go so that our friendship will not be broken.'"

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.

translated: They said to him: Let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily and follow me.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: «A b'a ka k'a a zibəəro mɩŋŋɔɔ ba, kʊ gʊɔɔ n dɩga a ma y. K'awɔɔ ŋ ba kʊ ŋ nɩ dɩga a ma, awɔɔ bɩ ganwʊrɛ yɩm awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ zi y.
translated: If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, neither will they be convinced if someone rises from the dead. Because if they do not listen to them, even if someone rises from the dead, they will not believe that God has sent him.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: B'ɩbɩɩ, k'ɩ ɩ gan wʊrɛ, k'ɩ jɩsa wɔ bɩ bɩ hɔ k'ɩ bɩsɩ wɔ n'a bam bɩ dɔ y.
translated: If you want to do something, you should think about it carefully before you start doing it.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: «K'awɔɔ k'a yaa da, k'a da ncɩnaaʊ: Wɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ, k'ɩ tɔ n lɛbɔʊrɛ yɩ,
translated: When you go, you should say: May God give you strength, so that you can work well,

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: K'ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ lɛ kʊsɩrɛ, k'ɩ hi da ɩ meer ʊ, ɩ tidəər zɛ ɩ mim ma,
translated: If you want to be respected, you should not show your nakedness, you should cover yourself,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: kʊ gʊɔɔ b'a dɔ, ŋ ʊ, ɩ lɛ ɩ kʊsɩ y. Bɩ, k'ɩ Zɩ k'a nɩ lɛɛ k'a nyaakʊm da ʊ bɩ n a dɔ a deem. B'a y'ɩ ganwʊrɛ karɛ ɩ ʊ.» Arzaka k'a nɩ arzana ʊ (Likki
translated: When you go there, you, you will see it yourself. Then, may God give you grace so that your heart will be at peace about what you will do there. May he give you safe journey there." Arzaka gave him provisions (Book

text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: If someone says to this mountain: Be uprooted and be thrown into the sea, and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will happen! Then it will be done for him as he says.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Bala, kʊ gʊaa hɔ yɛ, a y'a yɩm. K'a a ka ma, a y'a yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa cɛlɛ zɛ, n y'a gʊm nɩ.
translated: Child, if you do good, he will know it. If you do bad, he will know it. If you do evil things, I will beat you.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, mɔɔ ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ n awɔɔ dɔ fɩɩga y. K'a a hʊr mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, mimbʊnyaabənno!› Cɛ jɩr k'an mɩŋŋa (Likki
translated: If they ask you about it, say: "It is not your concern. Let him follow me if I want, you troublemaker!" That is what I said (Luke

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Wosocikarɩnsaamba deem n a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, mɔɔ zure ɩbɩɩ jɛ ʊ lɛɛ haay k'ɩbɩɩ nɩ taŋ ʊ b'ʊ.»
translated: When you meet a friend on the road, he greets you, saying: "Friend, I have been looking for you for a long time but I haven't found you."

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, hɩnhɛr gʊta wuti hi bɩ burə ʊ, an a ŋʊa k'a ka kʊ hi m kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ mom. Yeezuu n bɔkarɛ, an nɩ hinceem bam.
translated: Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as you did in the rebellion. Jesus is the apostle and high priest.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: A n bɔkarɛ, kurkuuro cir gʊta ɩ nyɩnta laatʊ b'ʊ, ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩrɛ.
translated: When we arrived, the old man was sitting under the tree, and he greeted us.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku har ʊ bɩ, bʊʊrɔ wɔɔ a zɔ ma, an ŋ lar, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ sɩra ka a ʊ, mɔɔ yɩ m dam ma, m naa ba ra?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩɩn…, Zuuba.»
translated: Jesus did not answer him at all, so the man stood up and asked him: "Who is my neighbor that I should love?" When he asked him that, he said: "Well..., listen."

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: A n a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Mɔsa zibəə bɩ gʊta, bɩ zibəənyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩ gʊta y.
translated: If you love me, you will say: "Moses' children are good, their children are also good."

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Danyɩnɔ kʊ kam zɛ ma. Zɩ y'a nyɩ karɛ, n n a zɛ. Nyɩnɔ wutire ŋnɩ jɩm kan a zɩrɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ ŋ ka, ŋnɩ ŋ zɩnzɛ.
translated: The world is not good for me. If you see it as good, then it is good for you. A person's character is what makes them who they are, what they do, and what they become.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Karɩndanyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a karɩnsaamba ra y, zibəənyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a zuuba ra sɔ y.
translated: Just as the child resembles its father, so the calf resembles its mother.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: N bɩr bɛnnɔ hɛɛnm hɩɩya wacɩr la? A n bɔkarɛ deem taan… bɩr gam k'awɔɔ Zɩ bɛɛ zaa ka y.
translated: What did you do with the money I gave you yesterday? You didn't buy anything... you just said you would go to the market.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: K'a maam, a bɩ nyi ba y. Awɔɔ gore ta ʊ, an lɛ da bɛnnɔ haay ra. (Likki
translated: If you refuse, it is not good. If you agree to it, we will be very happy.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: A b'a hɔɔn da, a ʊ, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ tara burə ʊ y. Mɔɔ n bʊr kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ y. Bʊr mɔɔ ʊ zoobarɛ m.
translated: If they come, uh, the person who has health and sees their friend who is sick. The one who has health sees them. The person is a visitor.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Kʊ gʊaa wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa si k'an wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa bɩ minto, a wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa ganwʊrɛ yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa gʊaa tɩrga si a tɩrgabaa tɔ ma, a gʊaa tɩrga ganwʊrɛ yɩm.
translated: If you call your neighbor a witch and your neighbor is not a witch, you the accuser are the witch. If you call someone a thief and that person is not a thief, you the accuser are the thief.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Zaan k'a nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ b'a to an Krista zibəəro wɔɔ mim ma, an a karɩndanyɩnɔ gɔsɩnnɔ nyɔɔ,
translated: When we were still sinners and had not yet believed in Christ, God's love was shown to us,

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.

translated: If God has chosen you and you are among them. Whatever you want to do in this world, you have the Spirit with you. And you will learn that I am with you.

translated: When the Pharisees and the teachers of the law saw this, they said to Jesus: "Teacher, your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath."

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Dʊdɔkarɛ hinni ʊ, Niniivɩ gʊɔɔ wutire, ŋnɩ her gʊɔɔ nɔɔn da lɛ ʊ, ŋnɩ ŋ gumə war bɩ hɩnka ŋ nɩ. Bala, Niniivɩ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ a bɔ a taam ŋnɩ sugur yɛ Zonas Woso lemim padarɛ ma. Bɩ naa ʊ bɩ, gʊaa ta ʊ an lɛ da Zonas ra.
translated: God had compassion on the people of Nineveh, because that great city, and its inhabitants were many. But the people of Nineveh believed God's word that Jonas preached to them. When it was like that, God did not do to Jonas what he said.

text: Hɔ k'a nɩ gasʊm gʊaa lɛ ʊ an ta a nɔ ʊ bɩ bɛɛ nɩ dintim kam ma y, bɩ hɔ k'a nɩ bɔm gʊaa lɛ ʊ bɩ nɩ dintim kam ma.»

translated: But, there are five types of diseases that affect it: rust, blight, smut, and also pests, and weeds too.

text: Bɩ, zinə ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, hɔ ba ʊ an b'a dam ma an a ka, an bɔ, kʊ yaadarɛ bɛɛ kan lɛkʊsɩrɛ kɩ y.]» Yeezuu a ya, an n'a zɛ mim dɩndam (Markɩ

translated: If a child refuses to listen to advice, let them go, and they will come back.

text: Yeezuu yɩrɛ bɩ zaa ba n nɩ: A da Maarii bɩ, nyɩnta a ʊ Zozɛf jaan, bɩ, an lɛɛ ka kʊ Maarii n a ba Zozɛf lʊ bɩ, *Sɛnt-Ɛspri a ka Maarii n jɩsɩ sa.
translated: Jesus' birth was like this: His mother Mary was engaged to Joseph, but before they came together as husband and wife, the Holy Spirit had made Mary pregnant.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: a nyɩ yɩm yar, k'ɩ tɔ ka Yeezuu, bala, a m n'a do bɩ bʊmbɔrɛ a mimbʊnyaarɔ nɔ ʊ.»
translated: "if you are truly the Son of God, come down from the cross and save yourself and us."

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: ŋnɩ laaka da: «Zɩɩfʊrɔ cir k'a yɩ b'ɩ ka? Wɔɔ monyaa yɩ dɔjɩr ʊ bɩ y'a ka, wɔɔ n bʊr k'ʊ b'a da ʊ.»
translated: "saying: 'What is the kingdom of God like? Or what parable shall I use for it, or what shall I compare it to?'"

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: When Herod realized that the wise men had tricked him, he became very angry. He sent soldiers to Bethlehem and the surrounding areas to kill all the boys who were two years old and younger, based on the time he had learned from the wise men. But the wise men had left, and they did not return to tell him where the child was.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: N zeedarɛ ma Ramaa ʊ, nyikəre kan heerzarlɛ gʊta kɩ: Rasɛl nɩ nyi karɛ a nyɩnɔ minto, a lɛ baa kʊ n n'a wɔ zɩnzɛ a nɩ y, nyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ gɩnga bɩ tɔ ma. Zozɛf bɔ Eziptɩ ʊ
translated: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning: Rachel weeping for her children, and she refused to be comforted because they are no more. Joseph in Egypt

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Zozɛf n wuti, an nyɩ bɩ kan a da bɩ kɩ gaa, an ta Israyɛl ʊ.
translated: Joseph is dead, and we do not know if his father will come, and we will go to Israel.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: A n aa nyɩnta kʊ kʊ n n'a hɩm ma Nazarɛtɩ ʊ b'ʊ, k'an bɩr yɩrɛ ɩ ʊ hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ a hɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ, ŋ ʊ: N y'a birm Nazarɛtɩ gʊaa.
translated: And when they wanted to take him to Nazareth where he was born, so that they could do to him what they had planned to do, he said: I will not return to Nazareth again.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Yeezuu k'a batɛm bɩ si bɩ, a bɔ hi b'ʊ. A m bɩ, brama n a gʊ, an Woso Ɛspri yɩ an nɩ zerle, am cirpʊʊrɛ hɔ bɩ m an bɩ zer la.
translated: Jesus said to them, "I am telling you the truth. Before Abraham was born, I am." And the Holy Spirit gave him strength, so that he could say this to them.

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Yeezuu k'a a ma, n ʊ, n Zaan du lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ bɩ, a a hʊr, an ta Galilee ʊ.
translated: Jesus rose up, and he, and John went out to him, he went, and they went to Galilee.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Sʊtaana n a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Kʊ Woso Nyɩ n'ɩbɩɩ m, k'ɩ ka kʊ jaarɔ nɔɔn duro n a ba hɔbɩrɛ.»
translated: "Satan came to me, and said to me, saying: 'If you are God's Son, command these stones to become bread.'"

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: K'a ta lɛɛ bɩ, a Zakkɩ yɩ kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ, Zebedee nyɩnɔ m ŋn'ɩ kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ kan a zɩ Zebedee bɩ kɩ, ŋn'a zaŋŋarɔ bankam. A n ŋ bir.
translated: When he went on from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, who were in the boat mending their nets.

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: «Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m mɩŋŋazerlezannɔ m: Ŋ hɔ m Woso cirbəə bɩ m.
translated: I will not be ashamed or disgraced: Because my God will help me.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m nyikərezannɔ m: Woso bra barɛ ŋ nɩ.
translated: I don't want to see you or talk to you: Don't come near me.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, a b'a wer si fɩɩga y: A b'a wer si brama tɔ nɩ y, bala, Woso cirbəəduu m bɩ.
translated: If you do not believe me, you will not understand this: You will not understand it at all, because, I am God's messenger.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: «Durnya yɛ n'awɔɔ m. Bɩ, kʊ yɛ nyɩntabaa bɔ, n y'a bam lɔ, k'a a ya, a nyɩntabaa ka ʊ? A zibəə ba ʊ dɔ y, a lo n nawʊm garga ra, gʊɔɔ nɩ tʊntɔm la.»
translated: "The world is in my hands. If it is a person's child, I will take care of them, so that they grow, is it not a person's child? Their parents are not there, they left me with responsibility, both big and small."

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ ɩbɩɩ m: Ɩbɩɩ bɩ bɔm b'ʊ k'ɩbɩɩ n ɩ zɩm bɩ ka haay y, bɩ tanka deem taan… bɩ gɔɔtarɛ y.» Jaandarɛ mim
translated: The person who said this to me: The things that you do so that the things I do are not good, they are very bad... they are useless." Police officer

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: «N y'a ya, n n a hɩ sɔ, n ʊ: ‹Kʊ gʊaa la a lʊ ra k'an wɔsaalʊmaci ka ʊ.›
translated: If I had known, I would have told you: 'Don't go there because our enemies are there.'

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: K'a a jɩnnɔ ŋʊa, k'a yaa da Woso m gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ fɩr karɛ awɔɔ ma rɔ wɔɔ tɔ ma.
translated: They said to him: Let him drink water, let him go to God's house so that we can see what he will do there.

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: K'a maam, k'a yɩ mɩŋŋa paan… amba awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ n mɩŋŋa paan… bɩ m.»
translated: If you want, if you want to go... but God if you have given me the ability to go... they say.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: «A b'a ka k'a a zibəəro mɩŋŋɔɔ ba, kʊ gʊɔɔ n dɩga a ma y. K'awɔɔ ŋ ba kʊ ŋ nɩ dɩga a ma, awɔɔ bɩ ganwʊrɛ yɩm awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ zi y.
translated: If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead. Because if they do not listen to them, even if someone rises from the dead, they will not believe.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: B'ɩbɩɩ, k'ɩ ɩ gan wʊrɛ, k'ɩ jɩsa wɔ bɩ bɩ hɔ k'ɩ bɩsɩ wɔ n'a bam bɩ dɔ y.
translated: When you go to the farm, you should work hard so that you can get something to eat.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: «K'awɔɔ k'a yaa da, k'a da ncɩnaaʊ: Wɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ, k'ɩ tɔ n lɛbɔʊrɛ yɩ,
translated: When you go, you will say: The Lord needs it, and he will send it immediately,

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: K'ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ lɛ kʊsɩrɛ, k'ɩ hi da ɩ meer ʊ, ɩ tidəər zɛ ɩ mim ma,
translated: If you want to be a leader, you must first know yourself, you must be humble,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: kʊ gʊɔɔ b'a dɔ, ŋ ʊ, ɩ lɛ ɩ kʊsɩ y. Bɩ, k'ɩ Zɩ k'a nɩ lɛɛ k'a nyaakʊm da ʊ bɩ n a dɔ a deem. B'a y'ɩ ganwʊrɛ karɛ ɩ ʊ.» Arzaka k'a nɩ arzana ʊ (Likki
translated: When you go there, you, you will see it yourself. Then, God will make you know that your thoughts are not what he wants. When you see the truth, you will know it.» Arzaka said to Arzana (Book

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: K'a maam, a b'a heer kɔ a bɩr a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: Bɔ wɔɔ n'a bɩrɛ? Bɔ wɔɔ n'a mire? Bɔ wɔɔ n'a dure ʊ ma?
translated: When he arrived, he asked his wife about her pregnancy, saying: How is your pregnancy? How is your body? How is your stomach that I see?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: If someone says to this mountain: Be uprooted and be thrown into the sea, and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him! Therefore, what will you ask of me in prayer?

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Bɩ, kʊ Woso ɩ huu dum pooyaa bur ma ncɩnaaʊ, bɩ ta an a ʊ her kʊ sɛ n b'a bɩ booti m, a bɩ huu dure awɔɔ ma an lɛ da brɔɔ m na? Sɩrakarɛ poore zannɔ!
translated: So, if God does not spare the natural branches, will he spare you? Therefore consider the kindness and sternness of God!

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Bala, kʊ gʊaa hɔ yɛ, a y'a yɩm. K'a a ka ma, a y'a yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa cɛlɛ zɛ, n y'a gʊm nɩ.
translated: Child, if you want it, take it. If you don't want it, leave it. If you want it very much, I will buy it for you.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Dʊdɔkarɛ hinni ʊ, gʊɔɔ gʊta y'a hɩrɛ: ‹Zuuba, Zuuba, ɩbɩɩ tɔ minto, wɔɔ Woso lɛ si ʊ so ra. Ɩbɩɩ tɔ ma wɔɔ la zinəro ra. Wɔɔ hoserlo ba gʊta ɩbɩɩ tɔ minto.›
translated: When that day comes, many will say to me: 'Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many mighty works in your name?'

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, mɔɔ ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ n awɔɔ dɔ fɩɩga y. K'a a hʊr mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, mimbʊnyaabənno!› Cɛ jɩr k'an mɩŋŋa (Likki
translated: If they ask you about it, say: "I don't know anything about it. Let him tell you himself, you foolish people!" That's what we should say (Luke

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Yirbəə k'a da bɩ, n bʊr gʊɔɔ m gʊta zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ. Yeezuu meer ba bala an la zinəro wɔɔ ra, an yaabazannɔ wɔɔ waa haay.
translated: When the day came, I took my goat to sell at the market. Jesus helped me find a buyer for the goat, and my problem was solved.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Yeezuu k'a gasʊ Pɩyɛɛr har ʊ bɩ, a Pɩyɛɛr dində yɩ an nɩ wʊtam mɛmarɛ m.
translated: Jesus looked at Peter when he denied him, and Peter remembered what he had told him.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Wosocikarɩnsaamba deem n a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, mɔɔ zure ɩbɩɩ jɛ ʊ lɛɛ haay k'ɩbɩɩ nɩ taŋ ʊ b'ʊ.»
translated: When you meet a friend on the road, he greets you, saying: "Friend, I have been looking for you for a long time but I couldn't find you."

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma, ŋn'a gu, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Zuuba, wɔɔ yɔ! Kʊ m bɛɛ m wɔɔ gɩngam!»
translated: "When the woman saw me, she ran, she came to me, she said to me: 'Zuuba, it's you! I thought you were dead!'"

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, hɩnhɛr gʊta wuti hi bɩ burə ʊ, an a ŋʊa k'a ka kʊ hi m kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ mom. Yeezuu n bɔkarɛ, an nɩ hinceem bam.
translated: Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as you did in the rebellion. Jesus is the apostle and high priest.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: A n bɔkarɛ, kurkuuro cir gʊta ɩ nyɩnta laatʊ b'ʊ, ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩrɛ.
translated: When we were young, the old people used to tell us stories, and we would listen.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Zinəro wɔɔ n'a yɛm Yeezuu han gʊta, ŋ ʊ: «K'ɩ la wɔɔ ra, k'ɩ wɔɔ nyɔɔ kurkuuro wɔɔ mɛ ʊ.»
translated: "The women who were with Jesus answered him, saying: "We have come to anoint his body."

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yeezuu k'a hɔ k'a nɩ ŋ heer ʊ bɩ dɔ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Bɔ minto, awɔɔ n'a hɔɔn darɛ ncɩnaaʊ?
translated: Jesus also asked him what his name was, and he said to him: "My name, why do you want to know my name?"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku har ʊ bɩ, bʊʊrɔ wɔɔ a zɔ ma, an ŋ lar, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ sɩra ka a ʊ, mɔɔ yɩ m dam ma, m naa ba ra?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩɩn…, Zuuba.»
translated: Jesus looked at him and loved him, and said to him: "You lack one thing, go sell all that you have, and give to the poor." When he heard this, he said: "Yes..., Lord."

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: A n a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Mɔsa zibəə bɩ gʊta, bɩ zibəənyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩ gʊta y.
translated: If they do not listen to you, you say: "Moses' generation refused, their generation also refused."

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Danyɩnɔ kʊ kam zɛ ma. Zɩ y'a nyɩ karɛ, n n a zɛ. Nyɩnɔ wutire ŋnɩ jɩm kan a zɩrɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ ŋ ka, ŋnɩ ŋ zɩnzɛ.
translated: The world is not good for me. If you see it well, I will see it. The person who has a good heart, he eats, he drinks, he is happy.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Ŋ tarɛ awɔɔ m kʊrɔ lɛɛzannɔ kan cinno kɩ taa mɔɔ tɔ ma. Naa nɩ nyɩntam kasɛtɩ ŋ mɩŋŋɔɔ kan kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ kɩ lɛɛ.
translated: I want you to help me find work so that I can take care of my mother. If not, swear on the fetish that you will give me money to use.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Bala, awɔɔ bɛɛ n nɩ meer barɛ a zim y, awɔɔ Zɩ Sɛnt-Ɛspri n nɩ meer k'awɔɔ n n'a bam bɩ darɛ awɔɔ lɛ ʊ.
translated: But, if you do not have the strength yourself, if the Holy Spirit does not help you so that you can know it, you will not understand it.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Karɩndanyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a karɩnsaamba ra y, zibəənyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a zuuba ra sɔ y.
translated: Just as the rainy season has its time, so does the dry season have its time.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: N bɩr bɛnnɔ hɛɛnm hɩɩya wacɩr la? A n bɔkarɛ deem taan… bɩr gam k'awɔɔ Zɩ bɛɛ zaa ka y.
translated: What did you see in the dream last night? If you remember it well... tell me so that God may help you.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: B'awɔɔ, ŋ y'awɔɔ minkə bɩ lɛ dɔ.
translated: Father, I have done something bad.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: K'a maam, a bɩ nyi ba y. Awɔɔ gore ta ʊ, an lɛ da bɛnnɔ haay ra. (Likki
translated: If you do not want it, give it to them. The thing is not good for you, and it will not benefit you at all. (Proverbs)

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: A b'a hɔɔn da, a ʊ, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ tara burə ʊ y. Mɔɔ n bʊr kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ y. Bʊr mɔɔ ʊ zoobarɛ m.
translated: If they come, uh, the person who has health and sees their friend who is sick. The person who has health and sees them. The person is a visitor.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Kʊ gʊaa wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa si k'an wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa bɩ minto, a wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa ganwʊrɛ yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa gʊaa tɩrga si a tɩrgabaa tɔ ma, a gʊaa tɩrga ganwʊrɛ yɩm.
translated: If a person's righteousness is greater than another person's righteousness, that person's righteousness is good. If a person's work surpasses another's work, that person's work is good.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: «Kʊ gʊaa awɔɔ si, mɔɔ a m si. Mɔɔ si k'a ʊ bɩ, bɔzaa ɩ mɔɔ nyɔɔ b'a a si.
translated: If you want something, you will get it. If you want it and you don't get it, the ancestors will give it to you.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Zaan k'a nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ b'a to an Krista zibəəro wɔɔ mim ma, an a karɩndanyɩnɔ gɔsɩnnɔ nyɔɔ,
translated: When we were still in our sinful nature before we came to Christ, the sinful passions aroused by the law were at work in our bodies,

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: A m naa, Yeezuu n a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ nyaa bam kan kʊrɔ k'a hoserlo ba ʊ gʊta bɩ ŋ n a bɔ a taam nɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

translated: Jesus left the region of Tyre, and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, through the region of the Decapolis.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Yeezuu n a gaa zamaa bɩ jɩr ʊ, an bɔ m toore ma, an a nyʊkʊnnyɩnɔ dundu tikki bɩ tʊr ʊ, an lihi da, a nyʊkʊr ma, an a nɔ a nɛɛm ma,
translated: God will come to judge you, and he will ask me, and the good deeds that you did, and the bad ones, he will ask me, and he will see your heart,

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Yeezuu lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, a ʊ: «K'a dundo a zi, k'a ba laatʊ farizɛɛnrɔ nyʊnyɔɔ bɩ ma, kan Hɩrɔɔdɩ hɔ bɩ kɩ ma sɔ.»
translated: Jesus said to them, saying: "Be careful, beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod."
